headline: Is Rackspace (RXT) Quietly Recasting Its Cloud Identity Around Regulated, AMD-Powered Enterprise AI?
summary: Earlier in 2026, Rackspace Technology announced a memorandum of understanding with AMD to integrate Instinct GPUs and EPYC CPUs into a fully managed, governed enterprise AI cloud, while expanding AI collaborations with partners such as Palantir and Uniphore for regulated and sovereign workloads. This pivot toward operating AI at scale within core enterprise systems, supported by embedded platform engineering teams, signals a deeper focus on regulated, higher-value cloud and AI services...

headline: Why Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Is Up 17.4% After Cavanagh Extends Foundry And Becomes Channel Partner
summary: In late May 2026, Thomas Cavanagh Construction Limited announced it had extended its partnership with Palantir Technologies’ Foundry platform through 2035 to serve as the digital backbone of its construction operations and workflow execution. An interesting angle is that Cavanagh is now commercializing its Foundry-based tools to support Palantir deployments across the wider construction industry, effectively becoming a channel for Palantir’s technology. Against this backdrop of a long-term...

headline: Software stocks whipsaw amid AI fears, as Anthropic IPO looms
summary: The software sector is currently caught in a tug-of-war between profound optimism and existential dread over the future of artificial intelligence. After a brief period of euphoria, reality—and algorithmic trading—bit back hard, sending major software stocks tumbling in a sudden reversal. The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF fell 3%, wiping out half of the 6% gains it had recorded just a day prior. The bleeding was widespread across the industry’s biggest names: Atlassian plunged 8%, Hu

headline: Palantir, XAI, PayPal, and Robinhood stock analysis
summary: Shay Boloor, chief market strategist at Futurum, breaks down Palantir's strong earnings report and future guidance. He also analyzes Elon Musk's decision to merge XAI and SpaceX, and the implications for Tesla shareholders. Finally, Balor offers insights into the struggles of PayPal and the potential of Robinhood as a 'super app'.

headline: Palantir's role in UK public services branded 'unacceptable' by committee report
summary: Britain's reliance on US tech firm Palantir in transforming public services is an "unacceptable point of weakness" that could leave people's private information "at the mercy" of foreign actors, MPs have warned.  The Commons Science, Innovation and Technology Committee has published a report about the firm's involvement in bodies including the Financial Conduct Authority and the NHS amid growing privacy concerns.  Founded in 2003, Palantir is a data analytics company contracted by governments, militaries and other large organisations around the world.

content.title: Palantir's role in UK public services branded 'unacceptable' by committee report
content.summary: Britain's reliance on US tech firm Palantir in transforming public services is an "unacceptable point of weakness" that could leave people's private information "at the mercy" of foreign actors, MPs have warned.  The Commons Science, Innovation and Technology Committee has published a report about the firm's involvement in bodies including the Financial Conduct Authority and the NHS amid growing privacy concerns.  Founded in 2003, Palantir is a data analytics company contracted by governments, militaries and other large organisations around the world.
